title: Make file writes durable with temp-fsync-rename
description: Write to a temp file, fsync it, atomically rename over the target, then fsync the directory so a crash cannot leave torn data.
body: Use when a file must survive a crash or power loss intact (config, database, state file). Do not write in place or assume write() reached disk, because data sits in the page cache and a crash mid-write leaves a truncated or torn file. Actions: write the full contents to a temp file in the same directory, fsync the temp file, rename it over the target (atomic within a filesystem), then fsync the containing directory so the rename itself is durable. Validation signals include a crash-consistency test (kill between steps) always leaving either the old or the complete new file, never a partial one. Risk boundary: dropping the fsync or the directory fsync on a durability-critical path is a data-loss risk requiring a crash test before acceptance.
